title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaking proposed ceasefires ahead of Victory Day parade in Moscow – Europe live
summary_full: Ukraine suggested an indefinite ceasefire this week but it was repeatedly violated by RussiaBack to Moscow’s preparations for the Victory Parade, Reuters is now reporting that, in the latest signal of concern about the event, there is “a limit put on the number of foreign journalists cover it” as a result of “the change format of the parade.”The Kremlin also responded to a report in the Financial Times newspaper (£) that EU leaders were preparing for potential talks with Moscow, insisting that Putin “is ready to negotiate with anyone” – but will not initiate the process. Continue reading...
